behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Agentic because correctness depends on real model output content (signature fields), not just plumbing. A stubbed model can return a perfect fixture and hide signature mistakes. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is single-step output validation, not a chained multi-step loop ("generated function signature matches...").
behaviors.test_description: Call the real generation model with representative interface-constrained prompts; parse returned code signature and assert exact match on parameter names/types and return type against expected interface.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Integration because the failure boundary is module handoff/order control across orchestrator + test runner + attempt bookkeeping. Quote scoping to plumbing: "Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately...". Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (not System); assertion does not depend on model output quality (not Agentic); no required multi-agentic decision chain (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestrator with deterministic stub generator and stub test runner configured fail->pass->pass; assert calls happen in strict attempt order and third attempt is never invoked after first pass.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Workflow because it requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (generate -> test -> refine -> regenerate) and success depends on model decisions across steps. A mocked model or single-call check would miss real iterative recovery behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with real LLM on a curated function where first attempt is commonly wrong; execute real retry loop up to 3 attempts and assert at least one later attempt passes provided tests within limit.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Integration because bug appears at boundary from test-result module to prompt-construction module. Quote scoping to plumbing: "prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages". Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency (not System); this checks context transfer format, not model judgment quality (not Agentic); no multi-agentic chain required (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Connect real test-result serializer and prompt builder; feed failing test outputs with names/messages; assert next prompt payload contains all expected failure identifiers and message snippets in required structure.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Workflow because this validates cross-attempt context carryover and a second real model decision conditioned on prior failure text. Single-step Agentic is insufficient; behavior explicitly requires sequential agentic handoff (prior failure context -> next attempt content).
behaviors.test_description: Run live two-attempt loop with real LLM where attempt 1 fails; inject failure context into refinement; assert attempt 2 code differs materially from attempt 1 and reflects failure-targeted edits (not identical resubmission).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Integration because this is orchestration/bookkeeping across retry controller and result aggregator. Quote scoping to plumbing: "stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error". Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed (not System); not validating model output quality (not Agentic); no multi-agentic correctness dependency beyond counting/aggregation (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic always-fail generator/test-runner stubs; execute loop and assert exactly 3 attempts, termination after third, and error payload includes per-attempt test results for all three attempts.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Integration by explicit scope: "full non-agentic pipeline wiring ... with deterministic stubbed model outputs." This disqualifies Agentic/Workflow because model-quality dependence is intentionally excluded. Integration-brake: no required OS/network/subprocess realism (not System).
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline in-process with deterministic model stubs and real internal modules for execution/bookkeeping; assert expected transitions, attempt records, and final status across configured fail/pass scenarios.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Minimum is Workflow because behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step live agentic sequence ("generate, test, refine, retest") and success on a hard task after initial failure. Stubs would systematically hide real loop adaptation failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real loop against a known hard benchmark function with real model calls; require observed first-attempt failure and later-attempt pass within max retries, capturing intermediate artifacts for diagnosis.
